_comment: Unbundle detection pairs — real NCCI/CCI panel definitions. For the demo, ONLY 80053 fires (14 components on the bill vs. the panel code). Other panels listed for production extensibility. J verified 2026-07-18.

bundles.rule: If >= min_components component codes appear on the same date instead of 80053, flag unbundle; impact = components_total - bundled_price
Subset of CMP; if BMP components appear without 80048 or 80053. Lower priority than 80053 (CMP subsumes BMP).
Total cholesterol + HDL + triglycerides + calculated LDL. If >= 3 components appear on the same date.
CMP + CBC + TSH. All three must appear on the same date (min_components=3 prevents false positives on common CMP+CBC combos). Note: rarely billed as this is an uncommon panel code.

ptp_edits._comment: Procedure-to-Procedure column 1/2 pairs — separate from panel bundles. These flag when BOTH codes are billed same date/provider and column 2 should be denied. Seed pairs from origin/database-work (J, 2026-07-18); WS2 extension pairs appended below. modifier_indicator 0 = never unbundlable; 1 = a modifier could legitimately separate the pair (lower severity).

pairs.note: Chest 2-view includes single-view; cannot bill both
CBC with auto diff includes manual diff add-on
IV push subsumes IV hydration initiation (modifier may unbundle)
Cannot bill two E/M levels for same encounter (mutually exclusive)
ECG complete (93000) includes the tracing-only 93005; cannot bill both
Comprehensive metabolic panel (80053) subsumes the basic metabolic panel (80048)
Therapeutic IV infusion initial hour (96365) subsumes hydration initial hour (96360)
Routine venipuncture vs. capillary blood collection; a modifier may separate distinct draws
Lipid panel (80061) includes total cholesterol (82465) as a component
